title: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

seo.title: Classic Hummus Recipe: Creamy Tahini & Olive Oil Dip
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best classic hummus recipe at home! This creamy Middle Eastern dip with tahini, chickpeas, and olive oil is perfect for any occasion. Get the easy step-by-step guide.
longTailKeywords: classic hummus recipe step by step
hummus with tahini and olive oil recipe
seo.h1: Classic Hummus Recipe: Creamy Tahini & Olive Oil Dip
h2Headings: How to Make Classic Hummus (Step-by-Step Guide)
Tips for the Creamiest Homemade Hummus
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic hummus recipe garnished with olive oil, paprika, and fresh parsley, served with pita bread.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this incredibly smooth and flavorful classic hummus recipe, perfect for dipping or spreading!

description: Classic Hummus is a cornerstone of Middle Eastern cuisine, offering a velvety smooth texture and rich, nutty flavor. This traditional recipe combines creamy chickpeas with tangy tahini, fresh lemon juice, and aromatic garlic, creating a truly irresistible dip. Drizzled generously with extra virgin olive oil, it is perfect for sharing with warm pita bread or fresh vegetables.

ingredients.notes: or 1 can (15 oz) drained and rinsed
for drizzling, plus more for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Chickpeas
instructions.text: If using canned chickpeas, drain and rinse them thoroughly under cold water. For the smoothest hummus, gently rub the chickpeas between your hands or with a clean cloth to remove as many skins as possible. This step is crucial for achieving a truly creamy texture.

instructions.title: Make the Tahini Sauce
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the tahini, fresh lemon juice, chopped garlic, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt. Process for 1-2 minutes until the mixture becomes pale and thick. It might seize up a bit, which is normal. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.

instructions.title: Add Ice Water for Creaminess
instructions.text: With the food processor running, slowly drizzle in the ice water, a tablespoon at a time, until the tahini mixture becomes smooth and creamy, resembling a thick, light-colored sauce. This emulsification process is key to a fluffy hummus.

instructions.title: Combine with Chickpeas
instructions.text: Add the prepared chickpeas and the remaining 1/4 teaspoon of salt (and ground cumin, if using) to the tahini mixture in the food processor. Process for 3-5 minutes, scraping down the sides occasionally, until the hummus is incredibly smooth and light.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning and Consistency
instructions.text: Taste the hummus and adjust the salt and lemon juice as needed. If the hummus is too thick, add a tiny splash more ice water and process again until it reaches your desired consistency. It should be light, airy, and spreadable.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Transfer the finished hummus to a serving bowl. Create a shallow well in the center with the back of a spoon. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle with paprika, and garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately with warm pita bread, fresh vegetables, or as part of a mezze platter.

tips: For the silkiest hummus, gently remove the skins from the chickpeas after cooking or draining. This extra step makes a noticeable difference in texture.
Always use good quality tahini that is runny and light in color. A bitter or thick tahini will negatively impact the flavor and texture of your hummus.
Hummus can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. For best results, let it come to room temperature before serving and refresh with a new drizzle of olive oil.
article:
Hummus. This creamy, rich dip has captured hearts worldwide. More than a simple spread, classic hummus is a culinary cornerstone, a Middle Eastern appetizer brimming with flavor, history, and wholesome goodness. Made from chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and olive oil, it proves how quality ingredients create something extraordinary. Mastering homemade hummus is rewarding, yielding a healthier, fresher, and undeniably more delicious result than store-bought. If you've yearned to recreate that authentic, silky-smooth dip, you're in the right place. We'll guide you through the definitive classic hummus recipe, ensuring perfection.

## What is Hummus?
Hummus, pronounced "HOOM-us," is Arabic for "chickpeas." It's a creamy dip or spread originating from the Middle East, enjoyed across diverse cultures for centuries. Its presence in culinary traditions across Lebanon, Syria, Israel, Palestine, Jordan, and beyond is undeniable. It symbolizes hospitality, shared meals, and healthy eating. The basic composition: cooked, mashed chickpeas blended with tahini (sesame paste), fresh lemon juice, and garlic, often finished with olive oil. This simple yet profound combination creates a hearty and refreshing dip, a perfect balance of earthy, nutty, and bright flavors, making it an indispensable Middle Eastern appetizer.

## Ingredients for Classic Hummus
The magic of an outstanding classic hummus recipe lies in the quality of its few core ingredients. Each plays a crucial role in achieving the desired creamy texture and authentic flavor.

*   **Chickpeas:** The foundation. Dried, cooked chickpeas offer superior creaminess. Canned are convenient
